channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Five terrorists neutralised in intelligence-based operation in Lakki Marwat
item.description: In Lakki Marwat district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, security forces conducted a precise intelligence-based operation on Sunday, resulting in the killing of five terrorists, the Inter-Services Public Relat...

item.title: Iran Detains Individuals Suspected of Sharing Intelligence with Israel Amid Air Strikes
item.description: Authorities in Iran have arrested multiple individuals in several provinces, accusing them of passing sensitive information to Israel. In northwestern Iran, 20 people were detained for allegedly provi...

item.title: Kimi Antonelli claims first F1 victory as McLaren duo fail to start in Shanghai
item.description: Oscar Piastri’s tough start to the 2026 Formula One season continued at the Chinese Grand Prix, with both he and his McLaren teammate Lando Norris unable to start the race due to technical issues. Nor...

item.title: Netanyahu releases video to dismiss social media rumours about his death
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu shared a video on his official X (formerly Twitter) account on Sunday evening to refute widespread social media rumours claiming he had died. In the clip, Net...

item.title: Puducherry Assembly Elections: Model Code of Conduct Now in Effect
item.description: With the upcoming Puducherry Assembly elections scheduled for April 9, the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) has officially come into effect. The Chief Electoral Officer, P. Jawahar, informed the press that...

item.title: Review Highlights Limited Evidence and Safety Concerns for Melatonin Use in Children
item.description: Melatonin has surged in popularity as a sleep aid for children worldwide, driven by its accessibility and perceived safety as a natural supplement. However, a narrative review published in the World J...

item.title: Veteran Gospel Singer Toun Soetan Passes Away at 73, Tope Alabi Pays Tribute
item.description: Veteran Nigerian gospel musician Toun Soetan don pass away at the age of 73, and e don cause plenty sorrow for the gospel music circle. Gospel singer Tope Alabi take to her Instagram to mourn the woma...

item.title: Ondo Police Arrest Suspected Eiye Confraternity Leader Akinwale Tomiwa, Charge Him with Murder and Related Offences
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command don arrest one suspected cult leader wey dem dey find before, im name na Akinwale Tomiwa, also known as 'Bella'. This guy na the alleged leader of the Eiye Confraternity ...

item.title: Government builds Matete Bridge to improve travel and support tourism at Tarangire National Park
item.description: The government of Tanzania has been improving infrastructure at Tarangire National Park to make tourism smoother all year round. One big project is the 85-metre Matete Bridge, costing Sh1.1 billion, w...

item.title: Kashechewan Chief Retracts Resignation Request Amid Ongoing Evacuation Challenges
item.description: Kashechewan First Nation Chief Hosea Wesley has retracted his earlier call for the resignation of Indigenous Services Canada Minister Mandy Gull-Masty. The initial criticism stemmed from frustration o...

item.title: Ohakim Endorses Tinubu for 2027 Re-Election, Citing Achievements
item.description: Former Imo State Governor, Dr. Ikedi Ohakim, don publicly support President Bola Tinubu for him second term in 2027. Ohakim talk say Tinubu first term dey very successful for socio-economic, political...

item.title: Ramanathapuram DEO implements MCC rules, suspends grievance meetings, and monitors cash movement
item.description: Following the enforcement of the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) in Tamil Nadu from Sunday, the District Election Officers (DEOs) in Ramanathapuram and Sivaganga districts have introduced several regulati...

item.title: Environment Agency to Receive Expanded Powers to Combat Illegal Waste Activity
item.description: The UK government plans to grant Environment Agency (EA) enforcement officers enhanced powers to tackle the escalating issue of waste crime. The measure, part of the forthcoming Waste Crime Action Pla...

item.title: Residents Flee Bargaja Village in Sokoto Amid Repeated Bandit Attacks and Kidnappings
item.description: For Bargaja village inside Isa Local Government Area of Sokoto State, many people don pack their load and dey comot the community because of too much fear from bandit attacks wey no dey stop. These ba...

item.title: Government Officials Assist Filipinos Returning from Middle East, Palace Explains Marcos' Absence
item.description: Malacañang has clarified why President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. did not personally greet Filipinos returning home from the Middle East, following the ongoing conflict in the region. According to Palace Pr...

item.title: West Coast Eagles show resilience in second half despite heavy loss to Gold Coast Suns
item.description: The West Coast Eagles endured a tough start to the AFL season, suffering a 59-point loss to the Gold Coast Suns at People First Stadium. The Eagles struggled early, with the Suns establishing dominanc...

item.title: Arsenal Eye Premier League Title as Manchester City Slip Points
item.description: Arsenal fans are buzzing after their team opened a nine-point gap over Manchester City in the Premier League race. Mikel Arteta’s side beat Everton 2-0, thanks to teenager Max Dowman coming off the be...

item.title: UpGrad to Acquire Unacademy in All-Stock Deal Amid Indian EdTech Market Consolidation
item.description: Indian online learning platform Unacademy is set to be acquired by rival upGrad in a 100% share-swap deal, bringing together two major players in the country's edtech sector. The transaction was annou...

item.title: Ouaddou optimistic Pirates can still challenge for title after draw with Siwelele
item.description: Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou remains positive about his team’s chances of winning the league despite a disappointing 1-1 draw against Siwelele at home on Saturday. Siwelele took the lead ear...

item.title: Regional Trial Court upholds robbery conviction of two former Imus police officers
item.description: The Regional Trial Court in Laguna has affirmed the conviction of two former police officers, SM/Sgt. Daisy Diones and Pat. Rene Mendoza, for robbing a retired teacher in Imus City, Cavite, back in Au...
